name: Temporal
what: Durable workflow orchestration platform for long-running, retryable, stateful business processes in code.
solves: Reliable orchestration of multi-step business processes that must survive retries, outages, and long waits.
users: Engineering teams building complex, durable workflows in code.
fit: Powerful but easy to over-adopt. Hylono should start with n8n and app-native workflows; Temporal becomes interesting only when business-critical long-running workflows outgrow those tools.
runtime: Server cluster with history/matching/frontend services plus database and optional search.
components: Temporal server services, persistence DB, task queues/workers in application code, and visibility/search components.
deploy: Self-host Docker/Kubernetes exists, but this is serious platform infrastructure, not a casual utility.
deps: PostgreSQL/MySQL/Cassandra depending on deployment, workers in code, monitoring, and durable queue thinking.
datastores: Persistence DB plus visibility/search components depending on setup.
auth: Service auth and application-level worker/client auth patterns.
apis: SDKs for workflow code, gRPC APIs, worker/task queues, and admin tooling.
security: Workflow payloads can include sensitive business data. Access, retention, and namespace boundaries matter.
setup_path: Do not adopt before exhausting simpler orchestration patterns.
env: Persistence DB, service config, namespace/auth, worker endpoints, and monitoring.
persistence: Back up persistence DB and treat workflow histories as durable state.
operations: Server upgrades, SDK compatibility, namespace management, and worker reliability matter.
failure_points: Using Temporal because it is powerful rather than because durable coded workflows are clearly needed.
integration: Could eventually power long-running rental/service/billing workflows in code, but not before n8n/app code hits limits.
verify: Identify actual workflows that require Temporal-grade durability and code orchestration.
risk: High complexity; high payoff only for the right problems.
gdpr: Workflow histories may store personal data; retention and minimization matter.
recommendation: LATE / ONLY FOR TRUE DURABLE-WORKFLOW NEEDS
overlap: Partial overlap with n8n and application job queues, but at a different sophistication level.
codex_read_first: Read n8n limitations and workflow-complexity notes before proposing Temporal.
codex_best_path: Defer until there is a clear set of long-running, failure-sensitive workflows that simpler tools cannot handle.
codex_never_assume: Never treat Temporal as a default queue or cron replacement.

assets.name: Samples server repo
assets.notes: Archived; use only as historical reference.
